programme.title: King's Edge
programme.purpose: Makes the King's graduate premium real, visible, navigable and evidenced for every student.

projects.title: Curriculum Embedded Graduate Advantage
projects.summary: Builds the curriculum embedded foundations for programme-level graduate futures, meaningful experiential learning and an externally useful language of skills.
projects.detailSummary:
This project establishes the curriculum embedded foundations of King’s Edge. It works with programmes to understand, evidence and articulate the value they create for students’ graduate futures, drawing on programme data, student and alumni narratives, curriculum insight and destination evidence. This supports curriculum development, student articulation, recruitment and the later graduate premium evidence work.

It also develops King’s approach to high-quality experiential learning design within the curriculum, defining the typology, principles, operational expectations and future entitlement needed to make meaningful internship-adjacent experiences more visible, better supported and more consistently available through a student’s course of study.

Finally, it establishes a richer and more externally meaningful language of skills, using the Standard Skills Classification as a granular vocabulary that connects programmes, opportunities, students and graduate futures. The aim is a skills-rich environment in which students can recognise what they are developing, programmes can make that development more intentional, and King’s can connect curriculum, experience and future value more coherently.
projects.transformationClaim: Every student studies on a programme that can explicitly understand, evidence and articulate its contribution to their graduate futures, gives them meaningful access to experiential learning throughout their course, and immerses them in an externally meaningful language of skills they can recognise, develop and use.

deliverables.title: Experiential Learning North Star and Future Student Entitlement
deliverables.lead: TBC, Convener of the Experiential Learning Strategic Community of Practice
deliverables.summary: Defines King's position on experiential learning, including typology, design principles, operational support needs and a future entitlement ambition.
deliverables.problemSolved: King's has excellent experiential learning practice, but too much of it is buried, isolated, locally dependent and not consistently accessible or operationally supported.
deliverables.whatChanges: King's establishes a shared north star for experiential learning and a growth-readiness package that can guide curriculum and co-curricular opportunity growth.

components.title: Experiential learning typology
components.summary: Define the range of experiential learning across curriculum and co-curriculum.

components.title: Learning design principles
components.summary: Define what makes experiential learning meaningful, reflective, supported and evidenceable.

components.title: Operational and assurance principles
components.summary: Set expectations around risk, partners, student support, access, reflection, assessment and feasibility.

components.title: Future entitlement ambition
components.summary: Set a future-facing aspiration for what students should be able to access through a King's education.

steps.title: Convene and scope the Community of Practice
steps.summary: Establish the group through Education Cultures and Innovation, with Edge requirements in its remit.

steps.title: Surface practice and build the typology
steps.summary: Identify strong but buried experiential practice and produce a working typology.

steps.title: Define principles
steps.summary: Set learning design, operational and assurance principles by April 2027.

steps.title: Publish the North Star package
steps.summary: Produce the typology, North Star, entitlement ambition and requirements needed to mobilise growth by April 2027.

dependencies.label: Delivered through the Experiential Learning Strategic Community of Practice.

deliverables.title: Curriculum-Embedded Opportunity Growth and Assurance
deliverables.summary: Uses the Curriculum Framework audit baseline to drive growth and assurance of curriculum-embedded skills development and experiential learning.
deliverables.problemSolved: King's does not yet have a consistent view of where curriculum-embedded skills and experiential learning are strong, hidden, unsupported, uneven or inaccessible.
deliverables.whatChanges: The audit baseline is used to prioritise growth and assurance of high-quality, supported, inclusive and evidenceable opportunity within the curriculum.

components.title: Audit requirements
components.summary: Define Edge questions for skills, experiential learning, access, evidence, articulation and graduate premium contribution.

components.title: Curriculum baseline
components.summary: Identify strengths, hidden practice, gaps, unevenness and access issues.

components.title: Growth priorities
components.summary: Use the baseline to determine where provision should grow and where support is needed.

components.title: Assurance model
components.summary: Define expectations for learning design, access, support, reflection, evidence and quality assurance.

steps.title: Define Edge audit requirements
steps.summary: Specify the questions the Curriculum Framework audit must ask.

steps.title: Translate findings into growth priorities
steps.summary: Use the baseline to identify where growth would make the greatest difference.

steps.title: Establish assurance model
steps.summary: Define what good provision requires in learning design, access and operational support.

steps.title: Embed through programme enhancement
steps.summary: Use curriculum governance and programme enhancement to grow high-quality provision over time.

dependencies.label: Needs the Curriculum Framework audit as the baseline.
Needs the experiential learning North Star and assurance principles.

projects.title: A Co-Curricular Scaffold for Purpose
projects.summary: Supports students to develop purpose and ambition, then move towards it through meaningful opportunities alongside and near the curriculum.

deliverables.title: Experiential Opportunity Operating Model and Partnership Infrastructure
deliverables.summary: Builds the partnership, operational and learning design support needed to deliver experiential opportunities within and beyond the curriculum.
deliverables.problemSolved: Strong ideas for experiential opportunity often fail to scale because partnership, operational and learning design support are unclear or inconsistent.
deliverables.whatChanges: King's translates the Community of Practice typology and North Star into practical delivery infrastructure for curricular and co-curricular experiential opportunity.

components.title: CoP response
components.summary: Translate the experiential learning typology and North Star into delivery requirements.

components.title: Partnership infrastructure
components.summary: Strengthen routes to employers, alumni, civic, cultural, policy, community and sector partners.

components.title: Operational support
components.summary: Support placements, projects, live briefs, consultancy, challenges and related activity.

components.title: Learning design and assurance
components.summary: Provide templates, guidance and quality expectations aligned to the experiential framework.

steps.title: Map current support and partners
steps.summary: Identify partner networks, operational support, delivery routes and gaps.

steps.title: Translate CoP outputs into delivery requirements
steps.summary: Use the CoP typology, North Star and principles to define required infrastructure by April 2027.

steps.title: Build reusable support
steps.summary: Develop templates, guidance, partner-brokering routes and support models.

steps.title: Embed across curricular and co-curricular growth
steps.summary: Make the operating model part of how King's grows experiential opportunity across curriculum and co-curriculum.

dependencies.label: Needs the experiential learning typology, North Star and principles.

deliverables.title: Flexible Adjunct Experiential and Thematic Opportunities
deliverables.lead: TBC, Experiential Learning Community of Practice
deliverables.summary: Grows flexible experiential and skills-based opportunities, including thematic priorities such as enterprise, social innovation, civic engagement and global opportunity.
deliverables.problemSolved: Students need flexible opportunities that can respond to evolving purposes, skills needs and thematic interests without always requiring formal course redesign.
deliverables.whatChanges: King's pre-pilots flexible formats, aligns them to the experiential framework, and later moves to demand-led commissioning as Canvas insight matures.

components.title: Flexible adjunct opportunities
components.summary: Short projects, live briefs, skills intensives, mentoring, employer-connected work and hybrid formats.

components.title: Thematic priorities
components.summary: Enterprise, social innovation, civic engagement, global opportunity, research experience, policy, sustainability or culture.

components.title: Demand-led growth
components.summary: Use Canvas insight, skills gaps and opportunity appraisal to decide where provision should grow.

components.title: Framework alignment
components.summary: Align flexible provision to the shared experiential learning typology and assurance expectations.

steps.title: Pre-pilot flexible formats
steps.summary: Design and test early formats before the full CoP framework is finalised.

steps.title: Align formats to the CoP framework
steps.summary: Review pre-pilots against the CoP typology, North Star and design principles by April 2027.

steps.title: Launch thematic pilots
steps.summary: Pilot thematic opportunities with clear learning design and evidence expectations.

steps.title: Move to demand-led commissioning
steps.summary: Grow, refresh or stop opportunities based on Canvas insight, reach, impact, value and access.

dependencies.label: Formats need to align to the experiential learning framework once produced.
Canvas insight supports later demand-led commissioning.

projects.detailSummary:
This project builds out from the curriculum embedded foundations of King’s Edge. It supports students to develop a clearer sense of purpose, ambition and direction, connect that to their skills and future plans, and identify the experiences they want to build at King’s.

It then creates the scaffold needed to help students move towards those ambitions, with the operating model, partnership infrastructure and learning design support required to make co-curricular and near-curriculum opportunities coherent, accessible and recognised.

The project spans credit-bearing or otherwise formally recognised project, placement and partnership pathways where academically appropriate, as well as flexible enrichment experiences shaped around student purpose, skills needs and emerging institutional priorities.
projects.transformationClaim: Every student is supported to develop a clearer sense of purpose, ambition and direction at King’s, and to move towards it through a coherent scaffold of opportunities alongside the curriculum, from credit-bearing or otherwise recognised project, placement and partnership pathways where academically appropriate, to flexible enrichment experiences shaped around student purpose.

projects.title: An Extra-Curricular Invitation to Participate
projects.summary: Makes the wider King’s experience easier to find, enter and take part in, through clearer routes into opportunities, communities and student life.

deliverables.title: Digital Portal for Opportunity Navigation
deliverables.summary: Builds out the existing Eventbrite-like platform into the digital route through which students find, understand and navigate additional opportunities.
deliverables.problemSolved: Students can struggle to see the full King's offer, understand relevance or find opportunities at the right moment.
deliverables.whatChanges: King's builds from an existing opportunity platform towards a richer digital portal that connects opportunities to Canvas, skills, evidence and recognition.

components.title: Existing platform build-out
components.summary: Clarify what the Eventbrite-like platform already does and what Edge needs it to become.

components.title: Opportunity taxonomy and data
components.summary: Define how opportunities are described, categorised, updated and connected to skills and aspiration.

components.title: Navigation journey
components.summary: Enable search, filtering, recommendations and movement from Canvas into opportunities.

components.title: Digital integration
components.summary: Connect to evidence capture, recognition, microcredentials and the enhanced student record over time.

steps.title: Build from the existing platform
steps.summary: Clarify current platform capability and Edge requirements.

steps.title: Define taxonomy and data requirements
steps.summary: Specify opportunity data, categorisation and links to skills, aspiration, timing, format and accessibility.

steps.title: Develop the navigation journey
steps.summary: Extend the platform so students can search, filter and navigate opportunities.

steps.title: Integrate with wider Edge infrastructure
steps.summary: Connect opportunity navigation to Canvas, skills, evidence, microcredentials and the enhanced record.

dependencies.label: The portal is part of the wider Single Student App / Digital Portal work.
Needs Canvas logic for purpose and aspiration.
Needs the skills architecture for opportunity tagging and recommendation.

deliverables.title: Belonging and Participation: Physical and Digital
deliverables.summary: Builds participation, connection and belonging across campus life, digital spaces and less campus-present student groups.
deliverables.problemSolved: Students do not all experience King's as a connected community, particularly commuter, digital, working and less campus-present students.
deliverables.whatChanges: King's develops a joined-up model for physical and digital participation, making campus and community easier to enter and more worth engaging with.

components.title: Destination campus
components.summary: Low-friction, visible and inclusive reasons for students to be on campus beyond timetabled teaching.

components.title: Digital belonging
components.summary: Routes for digital, hybrid and less campus-present students to feel part of King's.

components.title: Inclusive participation
components.summary: Design around commuter students, working students, carers, disabled students, international students and students under financial pressure.

components.title: Partnership delivery
components.summary: Work with KCLSU, faculties, services, sport, music, faith, culture and student communities.

steps.title: Define the participation and belonging model
steps.summary: Set the model for destination campus, digital belonging and commuter-sensitive design.

steps.title: Pilot physical and digital formats
steps.summary: Test low-friction campus activity, digital community models and inclusive participation formats.

steps.title: Scale across campuses and groups
steps.summary: Expand models that work, with attention to students with different participation constraints.

steps.title: Embed into the annual student experience cycle
steps.summary: Make participation and belonging a planned part of campus, digital and student life delivery.

deliverables.title: Subject Societies and Student-Led Discipline Communities
deliverables.summary: Strengthens subject societies as student-led conveners of disciplinary identity, belonging, opportunity, alumni connection and peer community.
deliverables.problemSolved: King's has strong subject societies, but they are not yet systematically supported as part of the graduate premium ecology.
deliverables.whatChanges: Subject societies become better supported as discipline-level conveners while preserving student-led independence.

components.title: Discipline-level community
components.summary: Strengthen belonging and intellectual identity within disciplines.

components.title: Alumni and employer connection
components.summary: Create routes to speakers, employer contacts, mentoring and sector insight.

components.title: Department and Careers links
components.summary: Connect societies more consistently with departments, Careers, Alumni and opportunity growth.

components.title: Student leadership support
components.summary: Support society leaders as conveners, hosts, connectors and peer leaders.

steps.title: Map strengths and needs
steps.summary: Understand society activity, department links, alumni engagement, employer connection and support needs.

steps.title: Define the support model
steps.summary: Create a light-touch model for funding, events, leadership, department links and Careers / Alumni connection.

steps.title: Pilot discipline community partnerships
steps.summary: Test discipline-level community and opportunity convening with selected societies and departments.

steps.title: Embed as part of the Edge ecology
steps.summary: Make societies a recognised route into disciplinary belonging, peer connection, alumni insight and opportunity.

deliverables.title: Rhythms of the Academic Year and Student Experience
deliverables.summary: Designs the key moments, rituals and transitions through which students engage, reconnect, reflect, celebrate and move forward.
deliverables.problemSolved: Student engagement often depends on timing, but opportunities, belonging interventions and reflective moments are not always aligned to the rhythm of the student year.
deliverables.whatChanges: King's creates a more intentional annual rhythm for welcome, reconnection, opportunity windows, reflection, celebration and transition.

components.title: Welcome and early belonging
components.summary: Help students understand King's, connect with others and begin shaping their experience.

components.title: Transitions and reorientation
components.summary: Support students after assessment, between semesters and between years.

components.title: Opportunity windows and reflection
components.summary: Prompt students to explore opportunities, review plans and evidence development.

components.title: Celebration and alumni transition
components.summary: Recognise contribution, belonging, achievement and movement beyond King's.

steps.title: Map the student year
steps.summary: Identify moments when students need welcome, reconnection, purpose, opportunity, reflection and celebration.

steps.title: Design the Edge annual rhythm
steps.summary: Build recurring moments, rituals, prompts and opportunity windows across the year.

steps.title: Launch the annual rhythm
steps.summary: Test through welcome, assessment recovery, transition points, opportunity windows and celebration moments.

steps.title: Refine annually
steps.summary: Make the rhythm part of planning for student experience, communications, belonging and opportunity engagement.

projects.detailSummary:
This project builds the extra-curricular layer of King’s Edge: the wider student life, digital routes, communities and annual rhythms that invite students to participate in King’s beyond their formal course of study.

It makes opportunities and communities more visible and navigable, connects students with campus and digital participation, and supports student-led disciplinary communities as routes into identity, peer connection and opportunity.

It pays particular attention to students for whom participation is less straightforward, including commuter, digital, working, caring, disabled, international and financially pressured students. It also creates a more intentional rhythm to the student year, using key moments, transitions, rituals and opportunity windows to help students engage, reconnect, reflect, celebrate and move forward.
projects.transformationClaim: Every student can find, enter and participate in a connected King’s student life, with clear routes into opportunities, communities, disciplinary belonging and recurring moments that help them engage, reconnect, reflect and move forward.

projects.title: Articulating and Evidencing The King's Graduate Premium
projects.summary: Equips programmes and students to surface, evidence and communicate the future-oriented value students build through their course of study and wider King's experience.
projects.detailSummary: This project defines and evidences the King's graduate premium as an integrated account of the advantages students build through their programme, wider experiences, relationships, choices and future ambitions. It brings together data, student stories, alumni insight and employer validation to help programmes make more explicit the broad, future-oriented gains already embedded in their provision, while also identifying strategic opportunities to strengthen those gains where this would make the greatest difference. It helps students connect what they study with what they do and where they want to go, and supports King's to project that value with confidence to applicants, families, partners, markets and wider audiences.
projects.transformationClaim: King's and its students more assertively lay claim to the advantages a King's education confers, using data, evidence and external validation to make those advantages more explicit, strengthen them where it matters most, and help students recognise, communicate and carry them into their futures.

deliverables.title: Graduate Premium Evidence Packs
deliverables.summary: Builds the evidence base for the graduate premium at institutional, departmental, discipline and market level.
deliverables.problemSolved: The value of a King's education is too easy to assert in general terms and too hard to evidence in specific, audience-relevant ways.
deliverables.whatChanges: King's develops evidence packs that combine outcomes, destinations, stories, employer insight and distinctive King's experiences.

components.title: Data deep dive
components.summary: Graduate Outcomes, LEO, highly skilled employment, further study, salary, destination patterns and internal evidence.

components.title: Department story design
components.summary: Work with departments to articulate their distinctive graduate premium story.

components.title: Prototype evidence packs
components.summary: Build packs for selected departments or disciplines.

components.title: Narrative capture cycle
components.summary: Capture student stories, alumni cases, employer testimony and distinctive experience examples.

steps.title: Start data deep dive
steps.summary: Pull together outcomes and destination evidence.

steps.title: Design department premium stories
steps.summary: Work with departments on what their distinctive graduate premium story is.

steps.title: Prototype evidence packs
steps.summary: Build first packs for selected departments, disciplines or markets.

steps.title: Refresh and capture narratives
steps.summary: Establish regular narrative capture and evidence refresh cycles.

dependencies.label: Uses programme deep dives to strengthen evidence packs.

deliverables.title: Employer, Industry and Alumni Validation System
deliverables.summary: Creates a systematic approach to employer, industry and alumni validation of the graduate premium, skills priorities and opportunity agenda.
deliverables.problemSolved: Employer, industry and alumni insight exists, but is not yet consistently connected to a graduate premium validation model.
deliverables.whatChanges: King's builds an external validation system that tests the premium, refreshes skills priorities and informs opportunity growth.

components.title: Existing activity map
components.summary: Map employer boards, alumni groups, faculty advisory panels, Careers activity and partnerships.

components.title: Validation framework
components.summary: Define how groups test graduate premium claims and skills priorities.

components.title: Structured validation cycles
components.summary: Run validation with selected panels, departments, disciplines or sectors.

components.title: Annual feedback loop
components.summary: Feed external insight into evidence packs, opportunity growth, curriculum conversations and market activation.

steps.title: Map existing activity
steps.summary: Identify existing panels, boards and partnership structures.

steps.title: Agree validation framework
steps.summary: Create a common framework so existing activity can contribute to graduate premium validation.

steps.title: Run first validation cycle
steps.summary: Test the framework with selected groups, departments or sectors.

steps.title: Embed annual loop
steps.summary: Make validation a regular input to evidence, opportunity and curriculum work.

deliverables.title: Graduate Premium Engagement and Market Activation
deliverables.summary: Ensures the graduate premium is understood and valued internally by students and staff, and recognised externally by applicants, families, employers, alumni, partners and markets.
deliverables.problemSolved: The graduate premium only matters if students engage with it and external audiences recognise it as credible and distinctive.
deliverables.whatChanges: King's builds internal engagement and external recognition of the graduate premium through evidence-led materials, student engagement and market activation.

components.title: Student engagement
components.summary: Build student understanding of King's Edge, integrated opportunities, skills, recognition and graduate value.

components.title: Staff and delivery adoption
components.summary: Create shared language for departments, advisers, Careers, KCLSU and student-facing staff.

components.title: Applicant and market activation
components.summary: Use evidence packs for recruitment, conversion, international positioning and audience-specific messaging.

components.title: Employer, alumni and sector penetration
components.summary: Build recognition through advocates, stories, partnerships and sector engagement.

steps.title: Start student engagement
steps.summary: Build student appetite around King's Edge, integrated opportunities, skills and recognition.

steps.title: Develop internal activation model
steps.summary: Create shared language, prompts and materials for staff and students.

steps.title: Test market propositions
steps.summary: Use evidence packs to test applicant, family, employer, alumni and international propositions.

steps.title: Build sector and market penetration
steps.summary: Use employer advocates, alumni voices, discipline stories, campaigns and partnerships.

dependencies.label: Market activation depends on evidence packs maturing.
Sector recognition should draw on employer, industry and alumni validation.

crossProgrammeDependencies.title: Education Cultures and Innovation
crossProgrammeDependencies.summary: Provides the shared practice infrastructure King's Edge needs in order to grow experiential learning with quality, confidence and coherence.
edgeNeedsServiced: Experiential learning typology across curriculum and co-curriculum
Learning design principles for high-quality experiential provision
Operational and assurance principles for different models
Inputs into curriculum growth, co-curricular growth and opportunity commissioning
crossProgrammeDependencies.whatChanges: Experiential learning moves from a set of strong but uneven practices to a shared institutional model that can support growth, quality, assurance and access.

crossProgrammeDependencies.title: Curriculum Framework and Review
crossProgrammeDependencies.summary: Provides the main route through which King's Edge requirements are embedded in the academic curriculum.
edgeNeedsServiced: Identification of programme-level graduate premium contribution
Baseline for curriculum opportunity growth and assurance
Evidence to support programme graduate premium profiles and evidence packs
crossProgrammeDependencies.whatChanges: Edge requirements become part of mainstream curriculum review, avoiding duplication and embedding skills, experiential learning and graduate value in the core academic framework.

crossProgrammeDependencies.title: Single Student App / Digital Portal
crossProgrammeDependencies.summary: Provides the digital environment through which the King's Edge offer becomes navigable, personal and usable for students.
edgeNeedsServiced: King's Canvas functionality for purpose, aspiration and planning
Search, filtering and recommendation of opportunities
Links between opportunities, skills profiles and student aspirations
Technical support for microcredentials and digital badges
crossProgrammeDependencies.whatChanges: Students move from a fragmented opportunity landscape to a coherent digital route through which they can plan, find, access, evidence and articulate the value of their King's experience.
